value.name: Spatial Mismatch I
value.desc: 📖 Longqi Bay Excerpt → Longma Grinding Wheel Factory Zero-Acquisition Case + Landfill Siting Public Hearing Scene Immersion. Core Inquiry: Why Is There Waste? Why Does It Become Dregs?
topics: Longqi Bay: How a fishing village surrounded by solid waste reassesses its value
Longma Grinding Wheel Factory: Waste Asset → Golden Asset
Landfill Siting: Site A/B Geological vs. Social Game
Public Hearing: 5-Role Multi-Stakeholder Decision Simulation

value.name: Spatial Mismatch II
value.nameEn: Systematic Framework of Solid Waste Management
value.desc: 📖 Qinglong Lake Excerpt → Qinglong Lake Fragment + Waste Sorting System Comparison + Transfer Station Spatial Layout Optimization. Group Debate: Is Bin Removal and Consolidation Good Policy or Lazy Governance?
topics: Qinglong Lake: Full-Chain Tracing of Urban Waste's 'Drifting Journey'
Chengdu / Shanghai / Tokyo Waste Sorting Comparison
Transfer Station Siting: Social-Spatial Game
Class Debate: Policy Debate on Bin Removal and Consolidation

value.name: Technical Mismatch I
value.desc: 📖 Yangliuba and Liujiawan Excerpt → Yangliuba and Liujiawan Fragment + FGD Gypsum Building Materialization Case + Incinerator Startup Full-Chain Case. Technical Route Review Panel Scene Immersion. Core Inquiry: Why do technically elegant solutions fail in the market?
topics: FGD Gypsum: Ten-Year Struggle Among Three Technical Routes
Incinerator Startup Full Chain: R&D → Outsourcing → Customer Acquisition → Transport → Installation → After-Sales → SARS Boom
Triple Transformation Model: Technology → Product → Commodity → Business
Technical Review Panel: 5-Role Multi-Dimensional Comparative Decision

value.name: Technical Mismatch II
value.nameEn: Biological Treatment and Thermal Treatment Technology
value.desc: Food Waste Treatment Process Comparison (Aerobic Composting vs Anaerobic Digestion vs Feed Conversion) + Incinerator Full-Chain Deconstruction (Cont.). Group PK: Recommend a food waste solution for a city of 2 million.
topics: Food Waste Process Comparison: Aerobic Composting / Anaerobic Digestion / Feed Conversion LCA
Incinerator Full-Chain Deconstruction: Breakpoints in Customer Acquisition / Pricing / Transport / Installation / After-Sales
Dioxin Control and NIMBY Effect
Group PK: Multi-Scheme Comparative Analysis

value.name: Behavioral / Temporal Mismatch I
value.desc: 📖 Sanduoli Alley Excerpt → Sanduoli Alley Fragment + Cost Reduction Extreme Detail Case + Landfill Operation Optimization. Leachate Treatment Operational Crisis Response Scene Immersion. Core Inquiry: With the same equipment, why is Plant A's cost 30% lower than Plant B's?
topics: Five-Inch Cost Reduction Method: Equipment Dimensions / Consumable Dimensions / Operation Scenarios / Installation Scenarios / Bidding
Waste Pattern → Process Optimization: Every Inch Has a Cost
Leachate Treatment Crisis: 4-Hour Emergency Decision
3 Incident Cards with Stepwise Pressure

value.name: Behavioral / Temporal Mismatch II
value.desc: Landfill Anti-Seepage Design (30-Year vs 100-Year Standard Debate) + Landfill Gas Carbon Trading CCER Project Valuation. Carbon Trading Negotiation Scene Immersion: Buyer / Seller / Validation Agency Tripartite Game.
topics: Landfill Siting / Anti-Seepage / Leachate / Landfill Gas / Closure Full Life Cycle
Time Engineering: Discount Rate / Full Life Cycle Cost / Intergenerational Equity
CCER Carbon Trading Negotiation Simulation
Opportunities for Solid Waste Industry Under the 'Dual Carbon' Strategy

value.name: Information / Humanistic Mismatch
value.nameEn: When Engineering Meets Law, Capital, and Humanity
value.mismatch: Humanistic Mismatch → Engineering Remediation + Philosophical Remediation
value.desc: 📖 Aishaba Wins Baiyun Village / Boundary Breaker Excerpts → FGD Gypsum Brick Litigation Case + EIA & Carbon Assessment Editorial Department Case + Brand Franchise Case + Environmental Project Investment Due Diligence. Two Back-to-Back Scene Immersions: Moot Court (45 min) + Investment Due Diligence Simulation (45 min). Core Inquiry: Why can't a good engineer only know technology?
topics: FGD Gypsum Brick Litigation: How Technical Issues Become Legal Issues
EIA & Carbon Assessment Editorial Department: Manual → Web-Based → Mass Orders → Asset-Light (Waste Contracts → Cash Cow)
Brand Franchise: Brand Authorization / Technical Training / Equipment Supply / Operational Supervision
Interdisciplinary Analysis Framework: Engineering × Law × Capital
Moot Court: 6-Role Courtroom Debate
Investment Due Diligence: Technical / Financial / Legal / Market Four-Dimensional Investigation
value.novel: 📖 Aishaba Wins Baiyun Village / Boundary Breaker [S012, S014]

value.name: Cognitive Integration
value.nameEn: From Technical Thinking to Systems Thinking
value.desc: Review all eight weeks of cases, map the transformation from technical thinking to systems thinking. Open-book assessment + Personal Cognitive Restructuring Report (≥2000 words). Core Inquiry: After eight weeks, how has your thinking changed? Dual mainline curriculum ideology & politics wrap-up: Study Hard, Make Progress Every Day — looking back; Lucid Waters and Lush Mountains = Invaluable Assets.
topics: Eight-Week Review: Mapping the Transformation of Thinking
Group Presentation: My Biggest Cognitive Transformation
Open-Book Assessment: Independent Analysis of a New Narrative Text
Cognitive Restructuring Report: Qualitative Reflection on Eight-Week Cognitive Changes
Ideology & Politics Wrap-Up: Yong Jia's Exemplary Significance + Environmental Protection as a Green, Sustainable, and Rising Profession

value.desc: FGD Gypsum Resource Recovery: 4 Roles × Technical-Economic Multi-Dimensional Comparison

value.desc: Leachate Exceedance: 5 Roles × 3 Incident Cards with Stepwise Pressure

value.desc: Brick Litigation Trial + Incineration Project Due Diligence Back-to-Back

dashboard.header_sub: Student Check-in · Weekly Report Data · Peer Review · Interactive Messages
dashboard.pin_note: ⚠️ First-time use requires Formspree configuration. See 'First-Time Setup Guide' at the bottom of the page. · After configuration, replace the three Formspree URLs below with the actual addresses in this page's HTML.
dashboard.card_weekly_desc: View each student's weekly progress reports, difficulties, and self-assessments
dashboard.card_peer_desc: View peer expert suggestions and opinions, rating statistics, and improvement recommendations
dashboard.card_github_desc: Teacher-student technical discussions, Q&A, course feedback · Log in with a GitHub account to post and reply
dashboard.card_student_preview_desc: Preview the form interface visible to students · Confirm form fields and buttons are working properly
dashboard.setup_step1: Open formspree.io, click Sign In in the top right corner, and log in with your GitHub account.
dashboard.setup_step2: Click + New Form to create 3 forms: ① 'Student Check-in' — name freely · ② 'Weekly Progress Survey' — name freely · ③ 'Peer Review' — name freely
dashboard.setup_step3: After creating each form, copy its Endpoint URL (format: https://formspree.io/f/xxxxx).
dashboard.setup_step4: Open Interactive Community.html, find the 3 YOUR_FORMSPREE_XXX_URL placeholders, and replace them with the actual Endpoint URLs.
dashboard.setup_step5: Open this file Teacher Dashboard.html, replace the 3 card links YOUR_FORMSPREE_DASHBOARD_URL with your Formspree backend address (https://formspree.io/forms).
dashboard.setup_step6: Upload the modified HTML files to your GitHub Pages repository, refresh the website to start using it.
dashboard.setup_tip: 💡 Formspree Free tier allows 50 submissions/month, sufficient for small-class teaching. Upgrade to a paid plan if more is needed.

w1.page_title: Week 1: Spatial Mismatch I — From Waste to Asset
w1.header_meta: 4 Class Hours (3 Theory + 1 Immersion) | Supporting Objectives 1, 3, 4
w1.core_question: Why Is There Waste? Why Does It Become Dregs?
w1.core_answer: Solid waste — resources misplaced in time and space; struggling learners — potential misplaced in time and space; the undervalued — value misplaced on the wrong platform. From engineering technology to social sciences, the philosophical root of 'waste' all points to spatiotemporal misplacement. Knowing the cause reveals the method; with the method, all dregs and waste can be resolved.
w1.tag_asset: Waste Asset: Spatial Mismatch → Assetization · Case 01: Longma Grinding Wheel Factory Zero Acquisition
w1.tag_space: Waste Space: Spatial Mismatch → NIMBY-to-PIMBY Transformation · Case 02: Landfill Siting and NIMBY
w1.tag_time: Waste Time: Temporal Mismatch → Intertemporal Realization · Case 04 · Revealed Week 3-4
w1.tag_tech: Waste Technology: Technical Mismatch → Productization · Case 03 · Revealed Week 3
w1.tag_mode: Waste Pattern: Behavioral Mismatch → Process Optimization · Case 05 · Revealed Week 5
w1.excerpt_text1: On a drizzly day at Longqi Bay, with nothing to do, I happened to open an old file on my computer labeled 'Yong Jia.' Opening it, there was the first WeChat message she had sent years ago: 'Mr. Hu Ang, I would like to intern at your company, may I?' Twelve years had passed in a flash. I stared at the screen; I no longer remembered what the weather was like that day. Only later did she tell me that when she sent that message, her hand was trembling. [Machine translation]
w1.excerpt_src1: Source: S001 | Longqi Bay (《龙栖湾》) Vol. 1 · Ch. 1 | © Registered | QQ Reading
w1.excerpt_text2: Regardless of whether he taught undergraduates or graduate students, Old Hu Ang's course was always solid waste. When he started his business in his youth, he labeled himself an incinerator expert. Solid waste treatment and disposal boiled down to two words: either 'burn' or 'bury'... The old leader wanted to teach Old Hu Ang a lesson — to extract the residual value of the research institute's shell resources. [Machine translation]
w1.excerpt_src2: Source: S003 | Longqi Bay (《龙栖湾》) | © Registered
w1.kp_def: Definition of Solid Waste (Solid Waste Law): Solid or semi-solid substances that have lost their original use value or have been discarded. Key word: 'Discarded' — subjectivity.
w1.kp_principle: Three Principles: Reduction → Resource Recovery → Harmless Treatment (decreasing priority, harmless treatment as safeguard)
w1.kp_mismatch: Spatial Mismatch: An asset in a place with no market — it's not that the thing is useless, it's that the location is wrong
w1.kp_lifecycle: Life Cycle Perspective: From 'Cradle to Grave' to 'Cradle to Cradle'
w1.case01_title: Case 01: Longma Grinding Wheel Factory Zero Acquisition [S002]
w1.case01_p1: In 1998, the Longma Grinding Wheel Factory under Chengdu Engine Company was on the verge of bankruptcy. 12 workers, old Soviet equipment, saturated market. Old Hu Ang took over all assets for zero yuan — assets, debts, and workers all became his responsibility.
w1.case01_p2: In a year and a half, he accomplished seven things: business negotiation, installment payments, equity transfer, traveling to Henan to buy raw materials on credit, trading grinding wheels for gaskets, reformulating to cut costs by 30%, and driving a van across every hardware market in Chengdu.
w1.case01_p3: A year and a half later, the grinding wheel factory closed. He sold the van to pay severance to the twelve workers. But — those seven things, each one crystallized into reusable knowledge assets: negotiation methodology, intuition for cost control, hands-on feel for technical transformation, pathways for channel expansion. That year and a half was worth more than a business school degree.
w1.case01_p4: 'Defeated again and again, yet fighting again and again — lessons and experience are both valid forms of intangible assets.' The waste asset, through seven strategies of refinement, became a golden asset.
w1.case02_p1: The game of Landfill Site A (Longji Slope) vs Site B (Shibantan): · Site A: Superior geology (K≈5×10⁻⁸ cm/s natural impermeability), but close to residential area (2,400 prospective homeowners strongly opposed) · Site B: Away from residents, but with developed karst (Permian limestone / dissolution fissures), hydraulically connected to well clusters
w1.case02_p2: NIMBY Effect: Technical Safety vs. Public Perception — How to Move from 'NIMBY' to 'PIMBY'?
w1.scene_roles: 5 Roles: Government Planning Official | Environmental Expert | Investor Representative | Resident Representative | Media Reporter
w1.scene_decision: Decision Point: Should the landfill be built at Site A or Site B? How to respond to residents' NIMBY concerns?
w1.scene_events: Incident Cards: ① Third-party inspection report reveals karst risks at Site B → ② Resident representative presents a joint petition → ③ Media exposes 'siting scandal'
w1.scene_output: Transfer Output: NIMBY → PIMBY Assetization Pathway Design
w1.kp1: Legal Definition of Solid Waste and the Subjectivity of 'Discarded'
w1.kp2: Three Principles (Reduction / Resource Recovery / Harmless Treatment)
w1.kp3: Spatial Mismatch Concept and Assetization Remediation Pathway
w1.kp5: Solid Waste Management Legal System (Solid Waste Law 2020 Revision)
w1.kp6: Landfill Siting Principles and Anti-Seepage Standards
w1.section_sizheng: Dual Mainlines of Curriculum Ideology & Politics
w1.sizheng_study_title: Study Hard, Make Progress Every Day
w1.sizheng_study_text: Yong Jia, with average talent and scarce resources, knocked on the master's door, progressing from sophomore classroom to independent practice. What she relied on was not talent, but continuous learning. Her goal was clear — 'maintain an enterprising spirit,' 'cultivate toward a true, good, beautiful, elegant, intellectual, and respected social role,' from master's to doctoral exams, 'replicating the path of expert professors.' 'Who really believes that even with average talent and scarce resources, as long as you keep studying hard, you can truly make progress every day?'
w1.sizheng_study_source: — From the Preface of Secrets to Changing Destiny Through Education
w1.sizheng_green_title: Lucid Waters and Lush Mountains = Invaluable Assets
w1.sizheng_green_sub: Spatial Economics Interpretation of the 'Two Mountains' Theory
w1.sizheng_green_text: Longqi Bay Fishing Village 'Garbage Siege → Environmental Remediation → Value Reassessment': The same piece of land, after ecological restoration, completely flipped in value. From 'NIMBY' to 'PIMBY' is the process of spatial assetization. Solid waste management is not a losing business — environmental protection is a promising profession that can achieve 'Invaluable Assets.'
w1.sizheng_closing: Waste is not truly waste, it is merely a mismatch of time and space; struggling learners are not lacking in talent, but merely a mismatch of method and confidence. Knowing the cause reveals the method; all dregs and waste in the world can be remediated.
w1.source_s001: Longqi Bay Opening · Yong Jia's First Knock on the Master's Door · Longqi Bay (《龙栖湾》) Ch. 1 · QQ Reading
w1.source_s002: Longma Grinding Wheel Factory Zero Acquisition Case · Longqi Bay (《龙栖湾》) Ch. 156-157, 188 · Repository Registered
w1.source_s003: Landfill Siting / Old Hu Ang's Garbage Philosophy · Longqi Bay (《龙栖湾》) · QQ Reading
w1.selfcheck_1: □ I can define 'Spatial Mismatch' in one sentence
w1.selfcheck_2: □ I can identify which mismatch each of the seven actions at Longma Grinding Wheel Factory remediated
w1.selfcheck_3: □ I can map the solid waste facility distribution in my city
w1.selfcheck_4: □ I have found a 'Waste Asset' in my own project — something undervalued yet valuable

w2.page_title: Week 2: Spatial Mismatch II — Solid Waste Management System Framework
w2.header_sub: Systematic Framework of Solid Waste Management
w2.core_question: What does a bag of garbage experience from the kitchen to the landfill?
w2.core_answer: You threw it away, it didn't disappear — it moved from your space to someone else's. 60 kilometers, 5 transfers, 3 form changes. At every node, the garbage 'changes its identity.'
w2.kp1: Four-Category Standard: Recyclables / Hazardous Waste / Food Waste / Residual Waste — Sorting is not doing the government a favor; it's determining the ultimate fate of each bag of garbage at the source
w2.kp2: Collection Systems: Fixed-Point Collection / Door-to-Door Collection / Pipeline Collection / Smart Recycling Machines — V=Q×T×K₁×K₂/(ρ×N)
w2.kp3: Three-City Comparison: Chengdu (2021) vs Shanghai (2019) vs Tokyo (2000) — Tokyo took 25 years to internalize sorting into habit
w2.kp4: Transfer Station Spatial Game: Solid waste facilities are always built where land is cheap, residents are few, and power is weak — it's not discrimination, it's an inevitability of spatial economics
w2.case1_p1: Yong Jia tracked the entire journey of a bag of garbage: from a resident's kitchen → community drop-off point → collection truck → transfer station → landfill. She discovered that at every node, the garbage changed its identity — 'Private Item' → 'Public Burden' → 'Compressed Cargo' → 'Waste Awaiting Disposal.'
w2.case1_p2: Core Insight: The same bag of garbage continuously changes identity throughout its spatial journey — waste ≠ waste, it depends on which station it sits at in the spatial network. Systems thinking is not acquired by watching; it's grasped by following step by step.
w2.case2_title: Class Debate: Bin Removal and Consolidation [S005]
w2.case2_p1: Pro: Centralized drop-off = improved efficiency, reduced cost, easier supervision. Con: Walk 200 meters more, inconvenient for the elderly, missing the schedule means leaving it at the doorstep.
w2.case2_p2: No winner or loser, only inquiry: Behind every public benefit, some group's personal convenience is sacrificed. The engineer's job is to minimize the cost of that balance.
w2.section_sizheng: Dual Mainlines of Curriculum Ideology & Politics
w2.sizheng_study_title: Study Hard, Make Progress Every Day
w2.sizheng_study_text: Yong Jia's industry notes accumulated page by page — systems thinking is not learned from books, it's grasped by following step by step. Learning requires graded progression: from classroom → from papers → from the field.
w2.sizheng_green_title: Lucid Waters and Lush Mountains = Invaluable Assets
w2.sizheng_green_text: Civic Responsibility in Waste Sorting — Protecting Lucid Waters and Lush Mountains is not the government's job alone. Every additional 100 meters a resident walks is the 'spatial price' paid for the urban environment.

w3.page_title: Week 3: Technical Mismatch I — Technical Compliance and Market Rupture
w3.header_sub: When Technical Parameters Meet Standards But Commercial Logic Breaks Down
w3.header_meta: 4 Class Hours (3 Theory + 1 Immersion) | Supporting Objectives 1, 2, 3
w3.core_question: Why do technically elegant solutions fail in the market?
w3.core_answer: Ten years, three routes, two failures — all three technical routes for FGD gypsum met engineering parameters. A failed on price, B failed on the industry chain, C failed on market awareness. Good technology ≠ good product ≠ good business.
w3.kp1: Technology → Product: Requires: controllable cost + replicable scale + certifiable standards. Breakpoint: Route B cement retarder — industry chain misalignment
w3.kp2: Product → Commodity: Requires: price competitiveness + accessible channels + customer demand. Breakpoint: Route A building gypsum — price cannot compete with natural gypsum
w3.kp3: Commodity → Business: Requires: profit model + supply chain + brand trust. Breakpoint: Route C self-leveling material — can sell but cannot profit
w3.kp4: Full Productization Chain: Case 04 Incinerator: R&D → Outsourcing → Customer Acquisition → Transport → Installation → After-Sales — six links, any break causes the entire chain to fail
w3.case1_p1: 2008-2018, Yangliuba. Thermal power plant desulfurization byproduct CaSO₄·2H₂O (FGD gypsum), ten years of attempting three resource recovery routes. All three met technical standards — but Route A's product price couldn't beat natural gypsum, Route B was blocked by the cement plant, and Route C failed to adapt to the market.
w3.case1_p2: Waste Technology: Good solution, bad market — where did it go wrong?
w3.scene_desc: 5 Roles: CTO / R&D Manager / Finance / Marketing / Production. Three routes, none optimal across all dimensions. The panel's judge is not technical parameters, but the trade-offs under multi-dimensional constraints.
w3.section_sizheng: Dual Mainlines of Curriculum Ideology & Politics
w3.sizheng_study_title: Study Hard, Make Progress Every Day
w3.sizheng_study_text: FGD Gypsum's Ten-Year Journey — three failures, ten years of persistence. Technical breakthroughs have no shortcuts; a decade to forge a sword — what it takes is not talent, but continuous learning.
w3.sizheng_green_title: Lucid Waters and Lush Mountains = Invaluable Assets
w3.sizheng_green_text: How thermal power plant 'waste slag' becomes raw material for the building materials industry — FGD gypsum, from pollution burden to building material commodity, is the most direct evidence of 'Lucid Waters and Lush Mountains becoming Invaluable Assets.'

w4.core_question: The product is made — then what?
w4.core_answer: R&D on one incinerator took three years (full commercialization cycle: five years). But selling it — took five years. R&D → Outsourced Production → Journal Customer Acquisition → Long-Distance Transport → Remote Installation → Remote After-Sales. Six links, any one of which could leave a high-performance solid waste treatment unit stuck in the warehouse.
w4.kp1: Food Waste Process Comparison: Aerobic Composting vs Anaerobic Digestion — Technology × Economy × Environment × Society Four-Dimensional Comparison Matrix. Not about choosing 'which technology is better,' but choosing 'which is more suitable under what conditions'
w4.kp2: Incinerator Full-Chain Deconstruction: Six Links: R&D → Outsourcing → Customer Acquisition → Transport → Installation → After-Sales. Every link was once a bottleneck
w4.kp3: Pricing Strategy: Competitors sold at 20,000, he sold at 200,000 — adding a zero. High price = psychological suggestion of high quality; customers buy security, not sheet metal at cost
w4.kp4: Life Cycle Perspective: Plastic takeout container: used for 30 minutes, degrades over 500 years. Designed in 3 months, consequences last 5 centuries — this is the 'temporal scale asymmetry' of the full life cycle
w4.case_desc: Why was Old Hu Ang's pricing effective? The furnace width of 1.6m just fit onto a truck — transport cost was already decided at the design stage. The control panel simplified to three modes — after-sales maintenance cost reduced at the root.
w4.pk_desc: Recommend a comprehensive food waste + incineration solution for a city of 2 million. Group A proposes composting, Group B anaerobic digestion, Group C integrated. Judging: Technical 30% + Economic 30% + Public Acceptance 20% + Presentation 20%.
w4.section_sizheng: Dual Mainlines of Curriculum Ideology & Politics
w4.sizheng_study_title: Study Hard, Make Progress Every Day
w4.sizheng_study_text: Incinerator's Five-Year Waste Time — from the grinding wheel factory closure to SARS, Old Hu Ang had no teacher, no course, no diploma. Bent over the desk night after night drafting blueprints, that notebook was his 'diploma.'
w4.sizheng_green_title: Lucid Waters and Lush Mountains = Invaluable Assets
w4.sizheng_green_text: Safe Medical Waste Disposal = Professional Social Value. During SARS, the incinerator was not just a product, but a public health defense line. The engineer's professional value was demonstrated at an extreme moment.

w5.page_title: Week 5: Behavioral Mismatch I — Process Optimization
w5.header_meta: 4 Class Hours (3 Theory + 1 Immersion) | Supporting Objectives 1, 2, 3
w5.core_question: With the same equipment and process, why is Plant A's cost 30% lower than Plant B's?
w5.core_answer: The answer is not in the financial statements — it's in the dimensions. Furnace width 1.8m → 1.6m, fitting two more units per truck. Filter replacement every 2 weeks → every 4 weeks, based on actual measured flux. Control panel 32 parameters → 3 modes, reducing service trips by 85%.
w5.kp1: Equipment Dimensions → Transport Cost: Those 20cm on the design drawing consumed a year's transport profit. Design is logistics
w5.kp2: Consumable Dimensions → O&M Cost: 'Manufacturer Recommended' ≠ 'Optimal Cycle' — actual measured residual flux determines replacement frequency
w5.kp3: Operation Scenarios → Labor Cost: The operator only needs to press one button — ergonomics = cost engineering
w5.kp4: Installation / Bidding: Pre-shipment ten-item checklist: nine days shortened to six. Profits concentrated in spare parts line; equipment body priced near cost
w5.case01_desc: Sanduoli Alley's Lean Cost Management Philosophy — Old Hu Ang, from one grinding wheel factory to one incinerator, decomposed cost reduction to centimeters, millimeters, and every minute. 'True cost reduction is drawing one more line or one less line on the blueprint — that line is worth hundreds of thousands.'
w5.case01_tagline: Waste Pattern: Every inch is leaking money — change the focal length, and every inch can squeeze out profit.
w5.scene_title: Scene: The Night of the Leachate Crisis [S011]
w5.scene_desc: 2:00 AM COD suddenly spikes → 3:30 AM critical pump failure → 4:45 AM surprise EPA inspection. Four hours, three emergencies, every step — repair or replace? Report or conceal?
w5.section_sizheng: Dual Mainlines of Curriculum Ideology & Politics
w5.sizheng_study_title: Study Hard, Make Progress Every Day
w5.sizheng_study_text: From 'Experience-Based Operation' to 'Data-Driven' — continuous improvement is making progress every day. Cost reduction is not a one-time action; it's PDCA day after day.
w5.sizheng_green_title: Lucid Waters and Lush Mountains = Invaluable Assets
w5.sizheng_green_text: Lean Thinking = Resource Conservation. Every ton of reagent saved, every kilowatt-hour conserved, not only saves cost but also reduces the environmental burden.

w6.page_title: Week 6: Behavioral Mismatch II — Landfills and Carbon Trading
w6.header_sub: Landfill Full Life Cycle and Carbon Trading
w6.header_meta: 4 Class Hours (3 Theory + 1 Immersion) | Supporting Objectives 1, 2, 3
w6.core_question: The Life of a Landfill — Who Pays for 30 Years Later?
w6.core_answer: You're 30 today, designing a landfill. 30 years later it's still under monitoring, you're 60. Spending an extra 1 million today on 100-year standard anti-seepage will let your 60-year-old self sleep soundly. Do you spend that 1 million? — The discount rate says it's not worth it, but conscience says groundwater is irreversible.
w6.section_framework: Framework — Engineering Decisions in the Time Dimension
w6.kp1: Landfill Anti-Seepage System: HDPE (≥1.5mm) + GCL + Compacted Clay → K≤10⁻⁷. The weak link is not the material, but the weld seams. The most expensive membrane is useless if poorly welded
w6.kp2: Landfill Gas (LFG): Scholl Canyon Model: Q=k×L₀×M×e⁻ᵏᵗ. Peak gas production at years 3-5, lasting 20-30 years — still 'working' after burial
w6.kp3: CCER Carbon Trading: 'Additionality' Three-Step Argument: Is it legally mandatory → Is it common practice in the industry → Are there investment barriers. Emission reductions are not calculated — they are argued
w6.kp4: 30-Year vs 100-Year: LCC = Construction + Operation + Closure + 30-Year Monitoring. The discount rate discounts future money — but groundwater remediation costs are not discounted
w6.scene_desc: 3 Roles: Project Owner / Carbon Asset Management Company / Validation Agency. Seller quotes 80 yuan/ton, buyer counters at 50 — is additionality objective fact or narrative construction? Incident: Ministry of Environmental Protection new regulation draft — landfill gas collection may become mandatory. Issued but untraded CCERs face cancellation risk. Carbon price is not simply a calculated product, but the result of multi-party negotiation.
w6.section_sizheng: Dual Mainlines of Curriculum Ideology & Politics
w6.sizheng_study_title: Study Hard, Make Progress Every Day
w6.sizheng_study_text: Carbon Trading = The Courage of Cross-Disciplinary Learning. Environmental Engineering + Finance + Law — entering a field you've never touched is itself the practice of 'making progress every day.'
w6.sizheng_green_title: Lucid Waters and Lush Mountains = Invaluable Assets
w6.sizheng_green_text: 'Dual Carbon' Strategy = Economic Realization of Lucid Waters and Lush Mountains. CCER gives landfill gas a price — it's not a slogan, it's calculable and tradable.

w7.page_title: Week 7: Humanistic Mismatch — Engineering × Law × Business
w7.header_sub: When Engineering Meets Law, Business, and Capital
w7.header_meta: 4 Class Hours (2 Theory + 2 Immersion) | Supporting Objectives 1, 2, 3, 4
w7.core_question: Why can't a good engineer only know technology?
w7.core_answer: Because your inspection report may be dismantled in court, your product manual may become future court evidence, your industry demo PPT may be used against you by opposing counsel in court. Beyond technology, the law is waiting for you, business models are waiting for you, and people who can scale your capabilities are also waiting for you.
w7.kp1: Engineering × Law: Brick Litigation: Technical parameters are objective — but in court, they become three versions. The plaintiff's version, the defendant's version, the expert witness's version. Which one is the 'legal truth'?
w7.kp2: Engineering × Business Model: EIA & Carbon Assessment Editorial Department: Manual → Web-Based → Mass Orders → Asset-Light. The essence of waste contracts is not bad clients, but the rigid cost structure of the manual model
w7.kp3: Engineering × Capital: Investment Due Diligence Four Dimensions: Technical / Financial / Legal / Market. The essence of due diligence = discovering hidden truths. When you sell a product, you're promoting; when you do due diligence, you're deconstructing
w7.kp4: Turn Yourself Into a Product: Brand Franchise: Brand Authorization + Technical Training + Equipment Supply + Operational Supervision. Core message: 'Turn yourself into a product, package it as a replicable standard product'
w7.case_title: Brick Litigation + EIA Editorial Department + Brand Franchise [S012, S019, S020]
w7.case_desc: Three cases, three dimensions: Technology → Law (brick litigation), Process → Business Model (editorial department), Capability → Product (franchise). Together they answer: A good engineer cannot only know technology.
w7.scene_a_desc: 6 Roles: Plaintiff / Defendant / Both Attorneys / Expert Witness / Judge. FGD gypsum brick quality dispute — can your lab report withstand cross-examination?
w7.scene_b_desc: 4 Roles: Technical / Financial / Legal / Market Due Diligence Team. A seemingly perfect incineration project reveals its truth under four-dimensional deconstruction.
w7.section_sizheng: Dual Mainlines of Curriculum Ideology & Politics
w7.sizheng_study_title: Study Hard, Make Progress Every Day
w7.sizheng_study_text: From Learner to Producer — Yong Jia independently faces clients and drafts proposals. 'Ask her, she knows it better than I do.' The master's one sentence marks the apprentice's qualitative transformation.
w7.sizheng_green_title: Lucid Waters and Lush Mountains = Invaluable Assets
w7.sizheng_green_text: Engineering Ethics = A Professional Way to Protect Lucid Waters and Lush Mountains. Environmental engineers protect the environment not only with technical parameters, but also with legal evidentiary awareness and commercial contractual prudence.

w8.page_title: Week 8: Cognitive Integration — Eight-Week Panoramic Review
w8.header_meta: 4 Class Hours (2 Theory + 2 Assessment) | Supporting Objectives 1, 4
w8.core_question: After eight weeks, how has your thinking changed?
w8.core_answer: In Week 1, I asked you — Why is there waste? Why does it become dregs? What was your answer? Now, after eight weeks, the same eight words — is your answer still the same?
w8.kp1: Spatial Mismatch → Assetization (W1-2): Longma Grinding Wheel Factory / Landfill Siting / Waste Sorting. Waste = spatiotemporal mismatch, not an objective property
w8.kp2: Technical Mismatch → Productization (W3-4): FGD Gypsum / Incinerator Full Chain. Good technology ≠ good product ≠ good business
w8.kp3: Behavioral Mismatch → Process Optimization (W5-6): Five-Inch Cost Reduction Method / Landfill 30 vs 100 Years. Cost is hidden in dimensions; focal length determines conclusions
w8.kp4: Humanistic Mismatch → Remediation (W7): Brick Litigation / Editorial Department / Brand Franchise. A good engineer cannot only know technology
w8.tag_asset: Waste Asset: Longma Grinding Wheel Factory — Junk bought for zero yuan, transformed into gold through seven strategies
w8.tag_space: Waste Space: Landfill Siting — The Spatial Game of NIMBY Facilities
w8.tag_time: Waste Time: Incinerator Startup — How Five Years of 'Waste Time' Accumulated Explosive Power
w8.tag_tech: Waste Technology: FGD Gypsum — Good Technology Meets Bad Market
w8.tag_mode: Waste Pattern: Extreme Cost Reduction — Every Inch Is Leaking Money
w8.tag_contract: Waste Contract: EIA & Carbon Assessment — Manual Model → Editorial Model → Cash Cow
w8.yongjia_title: Eight Weeks of 'Making Progress Every Day' [S001-S021]
w8.yongjia_desc: W1 Knocked on the door for an internship → W2 Took notes page by page → W3 Faced confusion over three routes → W4 Studied blueprints in the workshop → W5 Checked 13-item acceptance checklist → W6 Participated in carbon trading → W7 Independently faced clients → W8 Transitioned from learner to producer.
w8.yongjia_desc2: Is Yong Jia a genius? No. She just improved a little bit each week compared to the last. Her goal is no mystery — 'maintain an enterprising spirit,' 'cultivate toward a true, good, beautiful, elegant, intellectual, and respected social role,' from master's to doctoral exams, 'replicating the path of expert professors.'
w8.yongjia_source: — From the Preface of Secrets to Changing Destiny Through Education: 'It is unknown how much exemplary significance Yong Jia's case of coordinating work and study planning and timeline management actually holds.'
w8.yongjia_quote: 'Who really believes that even with average talent and scarce resources, as long as you keep studying hard, you can truly make progress every day?'
w8.exam_title: Open-Book Case Analysis (50%) + Personal Cognitive Restructuring Report (50%)
w8.exam_desc: Open-Book: New narrative text → Identify mismatches → Remediation solutions → Multi-dimensional argumentation. Report: 2000 words → Pre-enrollment vs. post-eight-weeks → Most impactful case → Future application → 'Why is there waste? Why does it become dregs?'
w8.gift_text: Eight weeks ago, I asked you — Why is there waste? Why does it become dregs? Eight weeks later, my answer is: Waste is spatiotemporal misplacement; dregs are coordinate dislocation. Knowing the cause reveals the method; with the method, remediation is possible. Turn waste assets into golden assets, turn waste time into an accumulation period for explosive power, identify every inch of optimization space in waste patterns, transform waste contracts into high-profit businesses, and turn yourself into a replicable product. Study Hard, Make Progress Every Day. Lucid Waters and Lush Mountains are Invaluable Assets.

transfer.narrative_text: Teacher Z's undergraduate and master's degrees were both in non-UAV fields. After joining a vocational college, through an Agent-assisted self-study system and without external training resources, they independently built a UAV knowledge system, and have now become competent in teaching 10 courses and have been approved for 3 teaching reform projects. Their self-study pathway validates the transferability of the 'Narrate · Framework · Scene · Create' methodology in cross-disciplinary faculty development.
transfer.step5_desc: Agent-B assists with teaching reform paper writing and project applications

transfer_hub.footer_privacy_cn: The case studies on this page are based on authentic teacher and student growth experiences. Pseudonyms are used for privacy. All teaching data is used with permission.
transfer_hub.footer_privacy_en: The case studies on this page are based on authentic experiences. Pseudonyms are used for privacy. All data is used with permission.
transfer_hub.intro: The NFSC pedagogy, Agent-assisted system, and dual-portal architecture have been validated in solid waste engineering education. To examine their transferability across disciplines, levels, and contexts, the research group has conducted exploratory applications in six distinct domains, covering five non-overlapping motivation types — pedagogical level migration, career-driven skill transformation, quality-of-life pursuit, parent-child learning support, and in-service teaching enhancement.
transfer_hub.tagline: Knowledge without boundary. Method with constancy.
transfer_hub.card4_desc: Validating NFSC methodology at the junior high level: simplifying complexity and guiding learning through interest, adapting the university-level four-step framework for extracurricular English tutoring.
transfer_hub.card0_desc: The NFSC pedagogy was born in the graduate course on Solid Waste Pollution Prevention and Control. After validation, it was adapted downward to the undergraduate classroom for the first time: course redesign, online teaching plans, simulation question banks with A/B exam papers, graduation thesis supervision, and innovation project incubation — forming a complete evidence chain of graduate-to-undergraduate pedagogical level migration.
transfer_hub.card5_desc: An in-service junior high chemistry teacher embeds the NFSC methodology into daily classroom teaching, validating cross-stage transferability from higher education to basic education.
transfer_hub.table_footer: Six non-overlapping motivation types spanning the full life cycle, forming a complete argument chain for the transferability of the NFSC methodology.

transfer_smart.narrative_intro: Facing structural adjustments and employment pressure in the Environmental Engineering industry, two teachers in the research group explored two branching transformation directions. The post-80s and post-90s teachers are at a critical stage of career development - without transformation, the major risks marginalization, while transformation requires systematically rebuilding the knowledge system. The Agent-assisted system plays the role of a 'knowledge bridge' in this process.
transfer_smart.narrative_title: Environmental Engineering Professional Transformation: Dual-Branch Exploration in Response to Industry Restructuring
transfer_smart.page_subtitle: Dual-Branch Exploration - Agent-Assisted Transformation - Curriculum System Reconstruction - Transformation Pathways for Environmental Engineering in Response to Industry Restructuring

transfer_smart.step2_desc: Find mapping points between existing competencies and the new field (risk assessment -> emergency warning; environmental monitoring -> indoor environmental quality)
transfer_smart.step3_desc: Agent-assisted literature search, mapping the target domain knowledge landscape, precisely locating knowledge modules to be supplemented
transfer_smart.step4_desc: Agent-S deconstructs industry reports into teaching cases; Agent-3 generates courseware frameworks; Agent-I designs practical training scenes
transfer_smart.step5_desc: Integrate old and new knowledge into a logically coherent teaching system, validated through the first batch of course teaching

transfer_music.framework_subtitle: Zero-Basis Traditional Folk Music Skill Acquisition Model for Older Adults

transfer_music.knowledge_2: Research Report on Key Techniques of Breath Training and Music-Wellness Integration for Middle-Aged & Older Adult Zero-Basis Beginners of Bamboo Flute Traditional Folk Music (Copyright Registered)
transfer_music.knowledge_3: Community Bamboo Melody Wellness Teaching Practice Plan for Middle-Aged & Older Adults
transfer_music.map_a0_music: Bamboo Melody Wellness Course Objectives - Health Indicators Matrix
transfer_music.map_a1_music: Folk Music Scores / Health Data / Practice Logs Archiving
transfer_music.map_a2_music: Bamboo Melody Wellness Teaching Plan Framework Generation
transfer_music.map_a3_music: Breath Training Visual Courseware (breathing curve diagrams / embouchure angle diagrams)
transfer_music.map_a4_music: Segmented Practice Plan + Self-Assessment Scale Design
transfer_music.map_a5_music: Practice Effectiveness Tracking + Health Benefit Assessment
transfer_music.map_ab_music: Music-Wellness Integration Project Proposal + Thematic Report Writing
transfer_music.map_ai_music: Virtual Ensemble Scene / Community Music-Wellness Activity Simulation
transfer_music.map_as_music: Classic Folk Music Masterpieces Deconstructed into Segmented Teaching Excerpts
transfer_music.music_age: The learner is nearly sixty years old
transfer_music.music_failure: No utilitarian pressure, but may undermine learning confidence
transfer_music.music_physiology: Declining lung capacity, finger joint stiffness, muscle memory deterioration
transfer_music.music_strategy: Short frequent sessions + recording self-assessment + metronome assistance + slow-paced progression
transfer_music.narrative_text: Teacher H is an Environmental Engineering professor, not a music teacher. At nearly sixty, he began learning bamboo wind Traditional Folk Music from scratch - a choice unrelated to career, sustainable after retirement. What truly drives him is the curiosity about 'how an engineering mind acquires a purely somatic perceptual skill,' and the research interest in transforming folk music practice into health and wellness practice for Middle-Aged & Older Adults. From 'professor who teaches' to 'beginner who is taught,' from rational logic to breath perception - this identity shift itself is the most unique transfer Narrative.
transfer_music.page_subtitle: NFSC Methodology - Agent-Assisted System - Dual-Portal Architecture - Transferability Verification from Solid Waste Teaching to Traditional Folk Music Wellness
transfer_music.step1_desc: Lung capacity test, finger joint flexibility, lip muscle control - confronting the physiological limitations that come with age
transfer_music.step2_desc: Abdominal breathing -> embouchure angle control -> sustained tone stability training -> vibrato modulation - deconstructing intuitive skills into trainable micro-steps
transfer_music.step3_desc: 5-minute short frequent practice method (adapting to Middle-Aged & Older Adults' attention span and physical stamina)
transfer_music.step4_desc: Recording self-assessment + metronome calibration - using an engineer's precision to compensate for lack of musical intuition
transfer_music.step5_desc: Refining personal learning experience into a teachable, researchable 'Bamboo Melody Wellness' Framework
transfer_music.timeline_stage1_desc: A nearly sixty-year-old engineering professor selects an entry-level instrument among bamboo wind Traditional Folk Music (flute, xiao)
transfer_music.timeline_stage2_desc: Learning numbered musical notation from scratch, deconstructing score structure with engineering thinking
transfer_music.timeline_stage3_desc: Core stage. Abdominal breathing, embouchure control, sustained tone stability training - this is somatic perceptual learning completely different from academic thinking
transfer_music.timeline_stage4_desc: From simple etudes to classic folk music works, gradually building performance confidence
transfer_music.timeline_stage5_desc: Integrating folk music practice with Middle-Aged & Older Adults health and wellness, forming the 'Bamboo Melody Wellness' practice model
transfer_music.timeline_title: Middle-Aged & Older Adults Zero-Basis Traditional Folk Music Introduction: Breath Training and Bamboo Melody Wellness Integration
transfer_music.wellness_1: Lung Capacity Training -> Delaying Respiratory Function Decline
transfer_music.wellness_2: Finger Technique Practice -> Maintaining Fine Hand Motor Skills
transfer_music.wellness_3: Breath Control -> Highly Compatible with Meditation / Mindfulness Practice
transfer_music.wellness_4: Ensemble Interaction -> Reducing Social Isolation, Enhancing Community Belonging
transfer_music.wellness_5: Repertoire Learning -> Activating Long-Term Memory, Providing Sense of Achievement
transfer_music.page_title: Transferability Exploration: Bamboo Melody Wellness - Cross-Disciplinary Self-Study
